mnemonics: RTL: Register Transfer Language, the Lingua Franca for describing digital hardware operations.
keyPoints: A register transfer is the movement of data between registers, typically controlled by a conditional signal (like a clock pulse).
Register transfer operations are the fundamental building blocks for describing sequential logic and processor operations at the register level.
The basic RTL notation is 'Destination Register ← Source Register', indicating a transfer of data from source to destination.
commonMistakes: Assuming a transfer occurs on every clock cycle; transfers are conditional and only occur if the control condition is true (e.g., if (T1) R2 ← R1).
Forgetting that a transfer is a synchronous operation; the data is copied into the destination register on the active clock edge when the transfer condition is met.
